reasoning_text: To determine productivity per hour, calculate widgets produced divided by hours worked for each shift. Morning shift: 240 widgets / 8 hours = 30 widgets/hour; Afternoon shift: 180 widgets / 6 hours = 30 widgets/hour; Night shift: 200 widgets / 10 hours = 20 widgets/hour. Both morning and afternoon shifts have the highest productivity at 30 widgets/hour. Total output from all shifts: 240 + 180 + 200 = 620 widgets. The factory needs 650 widgets per day, thus it is not meeting its target.
